items.definition_en: A dollar is the basic unit of money in the United States and some other countries.
items.example_en: He paid ten dollars for the book.

items.definition_en: A euro is the official currency used in many European countries.
items.example_en: Many shops in Spain accept only euros.

items.definition_en: A pound is the basic unit of money in the United Kingdom.
items.example_en: This shirt costs twenty pounds.

items.definition_en: A yen is the money used in Japan.
items.example_en: I exchanged my dollars for yen at the airport.

items.definition_en: A rupee is the currency used in India and some other countries.
items.example_en: He gave me fifty rupees for lunch.

items.definition_en: A franc is a unit of money used in Switzerland and some other places.
items.example_en: She paid three francs for the coffee.

items.definition_en: A peso is the currency in countries like Mexico and Argentina.
items.example_en: The price is twenty pesos.

items.definition_en: A won is the basic unit of money in South Korea.
items.example_en: He spent ten thousand won on dinner.

items.definition_en: A lira is the currency once used in Italy and now in Turkey.
items.example_en: I need twenty lira for the bus ticket.

items.definition_en: A yuan is the main unit of money in China.
items.example_en: This T-shirt costs one hundred yuan.

items.definition_en: A krone is the money used in Norway and Denmark.
items.example_en: The bus ticket costs ten kroner.

items.definition_en: A ringgit is the currency used in Malaysia.
items.example_en: He bought juice for two ringgit.

items.definition_en: A shekel is the money used in Israel.
items.example_en: The apple costs three shekels.

items.definition_en: A real is the official currency in Brazil.
items.example_en: He exchanged dollars for reals at the bank.

items.definition_en: A dirham is the currency in Morocco and the UAE.
items.example_en: She spent fifty dirhams on gifts.

items.definition_en: A dinar is the main money in Jordan and some other countries.
items.example_en: One lunch costs two dinars.

items.definition_en: A lev is the basic money unit in Bulgaria.
items.example_en: One coffee costs two leva.

items.definition_en: A krona is the money used in Sweden and Iceland.
items.example_en: He paid fifty krona for the meal.

items.definition_en: A ruble is the currency used in Russia.
items.example_en: A train ticket costs one hundred rubles.

items.definition_en: A zloty is the main currency in Poland.
items.example_en: The ticket costs five zloty.
